title: Session 23: ANIMA Frozen Frame Inventory — complete enumeration of frozen elements

content.purpose: Compile a complete, structured inventory of every frozen element in the ANIMA W+I organism (src/anima_organism.py). No formal frozen frame inventory existed for ANIMA — frozen_frame.json covers Living Seed only (last updated Session 13). Five sessions of ANIMA work (18-22) have accumulated 51 constraints without a unified frozen frame accounting.

parameters.description: Seven tunable scalars set at initialization. All seven are now characterized.

elements.name: w_lr
elements.description: World model learning rate. Interior optimum confirmed at 0.0003 (10 seeds, inverted-U: 0.0001→+0.0819, 0.0003→+0.0948, 0.01→+0.0274). 3.5× improvement from initial canonical 0.01.
elements.reason_not_adaptive: MI-err structural decoupling: all I-based signals show <25% variation across 100× w_lr range. Interior optimum exists in MI space but leaves no detectable gradient in prediction-error space. Stage 2 vacuous, Stage 3 w_lr path also vacuous.

elements.name: tau
elements.description: Fast I (mem) accumulation rate (EMA). Non-binding in [0.1, 0.7] range.
elements.reason_not_adaptive: Non-binding: performance flat across [0.1, 0.7]. No adaptive tau rule beat fixed baselines (c033). K-dependent reversal (c031) means no consistent adaptive direction.

elements.name: tau_slow
elements.description: Slow I (mem_slow) accumulation rate. Introduced in Session 22 as dual-timescale extension. Boundary-optimal at 0.0 — all tau_slow > 0 reduces MI gap.
elements.reason_not_adaptive: Harmful: any tau_slow > 0 reduces MI gap (max -34.8% at tau_slow=0.005, d=1.19). Best value is the boundary 0.0 (no slow I). Slow I carries previous-signal residue into current processing, degrading sequence discrimination.

elements.name: gamma
elements.description: Signal coupling strength. Multiplies signal values in neighbor computation. Boundary-optimal — monotonically increasing to 3.0 (canonical upper bound).
elements.reason_not_adaptive: Calibration at boundary: optimum is at the parameter's upper practical boundary. Adaptive gamma cannot beat best fixed value (boundary-optimal precludes interior adaptive target). No local proxy exists for global coupling (c002, c003).

elements.name: delta
elements.description: State update blend weight. delta=1.0 → pure new state; delta=0 → pure old state. Boundary-optimal at 1.0.
elements.reason_not_adaptive: Calibration at boundary: monotone increasing, optimum at 1.0. State-output divergence is an anti-signal (c036). No Principle-II-compliant internal signal can locate the delta optimum adaptively.

elements.name: w_clip
elements.description: Clip bound for W world model weights. Applied after each update: w[i][k] = clip(w[i][k], -w_clip, w_clip).
elements.reason_not_adaptive: Non-binding: structurally active (weights clip at boundary) but performance-insensitive across meaningful range. Parameter activity does not imply binding (c026).

elements.name: noise
elements.description: Gaussian state noise magnitude, applied per-step to each cell-dimension.
elements.reason_not_adaptive: Cosmetic: prevents deterministic collapse but performance-insensitive to exact value. No systematic sweep needed.

structural.description: Hardcoded architectural choices that define what the organism computes, not just how strongly. Most are untested — no binding sweep has targeted them.

elements.name: ring_topology
elements.description: Each dimension k interacts only with its immediate neighbors k+1 and k-1 (modulo D, wrapping). This is the sole connectivity structure — no long-range interactions.
elements.notes: Stage 5 target (topology adaptation). Untested whether alternative topologies (fully-connected, random, small-world) produce better sequence discrimination. Ring chosen by initial design.

elements.name: cell_count
elements.description: Number of cells NC=6. Each cell maintains its own x[NC][D], w[NC][D], mem[NC][D], mem_slow[NC][D] state. Centroid is mean across NC cells.
elements.notes: Cannot adapt from within current architecture. Would require architectural restructuring (spawning/merging cells). Not a Stage 3/4 target.

elements.name: dimension
elements.description: State space dimensionality D=12. All arrays are NC×D. Signal is D-length. Centroid is D-length.
elements.notes: Cannot adapt from within current architecture. Tied to signal format and harness interface. Not a Stage 3/4 target.

elements.name: tanh_nonlinearity
elements.description: Activation applied in two places: (1) err-to-mem accumulation: _tanh(err), (2) state update: phi = _tanh(pre_act). Clamping at ±20 prevents overflow.
elements.notes: Stage 6 target (functional form adaptation). Alternative activations (sigmoid, ReLU-like, linear) untested. Tanh chosen for biological plausibility and boundedness.

elements.name: additive_state_composition
elements.description: State pre-activation is strictly additive: self-state + W term + fast-I term + slow-I term. No multiplicative gating or attention between components.
elements.notes: Multiplicative/gating alternatives untested. Session 21 noted I_slow/I_fast ratio <1.5% variation — suggests additive vs multiplicative coupling may not matter for slow I specifically, but untested for core composition.

elements.name: ema_mem_accumulation
elements.description: Exponential moving average form for I accumulation. Bounded by tanh(err) ∈ (-1,1). Forgetting rate = (1-tau).
elements.notes: Alternative accumulation forms (sum, max, gated) untested. EMA form chosen for stability. Stage 6 target — if functional form becomes adaptive, accumulation rule is a candidate.

elements.name: prediction_error_formulation
elements.description: Prediction error is linear subtraction: signal-modulated neighbor interaction minus W-predicted baseline. W acts as a one-weight linear predictor per cell-dimension.
elements.notes: Linear predictor chosen for simplicity. Nonlinear predictors or different error formulations untested. This determines what I accumulates — a Stage 6/7 adaptation candidate.

elements.name: signal_coupling_additive
elements.description: Signal enters via additive offset to neighbor state values before multiplication. Gamma scales how strongly signal perturbs neighborhood.
elements.notes: Gamma is calibration (c046). The structural form (additive vs multiplicative signal coupling) untested but gamma constraint covers the scaling. Alternative coupling forms would be architectural changes.

elements.name: w_update_rule
elements.description: W world model update is gradient-like: bounded by tanh, scaled by w_lr, additive. W clip applied after update.
elements.notes: Stage 7 target — if update rule becomes adaptive data, W's own update rule is a candidate. Currently hardcoded in Python. No self-representation mechanism.

elements.name: centroid_mean
elements.description: Centroid computed as simple arithmetic mean of all NC cell states for each dimension k.
elements.notes: Alternative aggregations (weighted mean, max-pool, attention) untested. Centroid is harness interface — changing would require harness modification.

elements.name: state_update_blend
elements.description: Linear blend between old state and new activation phi. At delta=1.0 (canonical), this reduces to v = phi — pure replacement. The blend form is retained for generality.
elements.notes: delta=1.0 canonical (boundary-optimal). Blend is structurally present but functionally inactive at canonical settings.

elements.name: initial_conditions
elements.description: Cell state initialized from N(0,0.5), world model weights from N(0,0.1), memory initialized to zero.
elements.notes: Initial conditions vary by seed (random.seed(seed)). Ground truth test requires robustness across seeds (3+ independent initializations). Not an adaptation target — varied by design.

architecture_ceiling_analysis.stage3_status: Architecture ceiling for Stage 3. All 7 parameters characterized. None is both binding AND adaptable from within via Principle-II-compliant signal. Stage 2 vacuous (c048). Stage 3 all paths exhausted: w_lr vacuous (c049), tau_slow harmful (c051).
architecture_ceiling_analysis.stage4_status: No Stage 4 structural binding sweeps completed on ANIMA. Ring topology, NC, D, tanh form, additive composition all untested. These are the next frozen frame reduction targets.
architecture_ceiling_analysis.stage5_status: Topology (ring) untested. Stage 5 target pending Stage 4 completion.
architecture_ceiling_analysis.stage6_status: Functional forms (tanh, EMA, prediction error formulation, W update rule) all untested. Stage 6 targets pending.
architecture_ceiling_analysis.stage7_status: No self-representation mechanism. W update rule and I update rule hardcoded in Python. Stage 7 requires hypernetwork H within I (c038). Forward viability check: PASSED (entry 064 — hypernetwork H within I is the path).
architecture_ceiling_analysis.frozen_frame_minimum: Cannot determine precise minimum without Stage 4+ sweeps. Current characterization: 7/7 parameters frozen (all calibration/non-binding/harmful — no adaptive reduction achieved). Structural elements largely untested. Architecture ceiling at Stage 3 for parameter space; structural exploration not yet begun.

content.verdict: 19 total frozen elements enumerated: 7 parameters (all characterized) + 12 structural (1 calibration, 9 untested, 2 not adaptable by design). Of 7 parameters: 3 are calibration-only (w_lr interior, gamma boundary, delta boundary), 2 are non-binding (tau, w_clip), 1 is harmful (tau_slow), 1 is cosmetic (noise). No parameter yields a genuine adaptive reduction. The ANIMA frozen frame minimum under current characterization is unknown — Stage 4+ structural sweeps have not been conducted. The next productive experiments are structural binding sweeps on untested elements: ring topology (Stage 5 candidate), tanh nonlinearity (Stage 6 candidate), additive composition (Stage 6 candidate), and the W/I update rules (Stage 7 targets). The Stage 3 architecture ceiling is confirmed. The path forward is Stage 4 structural characterization.
